title: SQLite Database Creation, Connection, and Transaction Flow
description: A flowchart depicting the sequential process of creating and connecting to an SQLite database in mobile app development, including the use of transactions for data integrity.
relationships: App Startup triggers the creation of an SQLiteOpenHelper class which calls onCreate() to build the database schema. The app then calls getWritableDatabase() to establish a Database Connection. For data integrity, a transaction is started with Begin Transaction, followed by one or more Execute SQL Operations. If all operations succeed, the changes are saved with Commit Transaction. If any operation fails, changes are reverted with Rollback Transaction. Finally, the Connection is Closed.
